title: Normalize exact source financial facts
description: Translate each qualified transaction-bearing record into the canonical LEDGER fields under the resolved currency, account-class, sign, and date rules while preserving provenance. Implements G2 and UC-INGEST-001.
rationale: Financially plausible substitutions are more dangerous than explicit rejection.
story_want: have statement records normalized exactly and account-aware
story_so_that: asset and credit-account movements mean the same thing throughout Tally
acceptance_criteria: Given a transaction-bearing source record with complete evidence, when it is normalized, then the candidate preserves the selected account, original description, exact base-10 amount, ZAR, transaction date, optional distinct posting date, adapter version, source fingerprint, source-context record identity, and source reference without precision loss.

Given an asset or liability statement movement, when its signed amount is normalized, then positive means the owner economic position improved and negative means it worsened: asset increases and liability reductions are positive; asset decreases and liability increases are negative.
Given a source transaction date that includes its year, when it is normalized, then that exact calendar date is the Effective Date.

Given a source row with zero monetary movement, when it is accounted for, then it is retained as an explicitly excluded non-transaction record with a stable reason and no zero-amount LEDGER candidate is created.

Given a missing or ambiguous amount, sign, currency, date, description, record boundary, account meaning, or provenance field, when normalization is attempted, then the source record is blocked without inventing or editing a value.

Given a row date without a year in the qualified yearless layout, when exactly one matching calendar date falls inside the explicit statement period, then that date is the Effective Date; otherwise the batch is blocked.
security_criteria: Normalized candidates and manifests are sensitive financial artifacts and must never be serialized into logs or diagnostic messages.
